new_classes.label_en: Other mineral water or aerated water, not natural mineral water (subheading 2201 10 90)
new_classes.label_de: Anderes Mineralwasser oder kohlensäurehaltiges Wasser, kein natürliches Mineralwasser (Unterposition 2201 10 90)
new_classes.definition_en: Mineral water and aerated water as referred to in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201, paragraphs (B) and (C), that is not natural mineral water within the meaning of paragraph (A); excludes natural mineral water in aerosol containers for skin care (heading 3304). Presented without added sugar, sweetening matter, or flavouring.

A quality borne by a water product indicating that it is artificial (man-made) mineral water or aerated water as described in HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201, paragraphs (B) and (C), as opposed to natural mineral water under paragraph (A).

A quality borne by a water product indicating that it is not presented as a natural mineral water in aerosol containers for use in the care of the skin (which would fall under heading 3304), and thus remains within the scope of heading 2201.

coverage_explanation: The principal distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) membership in the MineralWaterOrCarbonatedWater2201 class (covering HS paragraphs B and C); (2) exclusion of natural mineral water (complement of NaturalMineralWaterQuality, distinguishing this node from sibling 22011011); (3) exclusion of aerosol-container skin-care presentations (NotForSkinCareUseQuality pointing to heading 3304 exclusion). A minor gap remains: the HS Explanatory Note paragraphs (B) and (C) distinguish 'artificial mineral water' (artificially mineralised water) from 'aerated water' (plain carbonated water); these two sub-categories are referenced textually but not further subdivided at this 8-digit CN level, so a single class with the ArtificialMineralWaterQuality restriction sufficiently covers both without over-specifying. The reference to 'aerated' could additionally be captured via the existing CarbonatedQuality, but since the code covers BOTH carbonated and non-carbonated artificial mineral waters, attaching CarbonatedQuality as a required quality would over-restrict; it is therefore omitted. Coverage score reduced slightly to 0.82 to reflect this residual ambiguity in the aerated vs. still artificial mineral water distinction not formally axiomatised.
